title: King Charles III England Coast Path at Carnforth, Lancashire: proposals to change the approved route
body: "On 26 March 2025, Natural England submitted a report to the Secretary of State for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs setting out proposed changes to the approved route of the King Charles III England Coast Path at Carnforth in Lancashire, a section of the Silverdale to Cleveleys stretch.  Read the variation report to find out why changes to the approved route are required.   The opportunity to comment closed at midnight on Wednesday 21 May 2025. You can no longer make a representation or objection on proposals.   Objections will be passed to an independent planning inspector who will make a recommendation to the Secretary of State about each one.  The Secretary of State will consider all representations and objections before making a decision about Natural England’s proposals.  Comment on Natural England’s proposals  View the guidance on representations and objections. "
